stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is text generation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of transformer-based text generation models.
The associated tags indicate relevance to major AI frameworks like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Inside the fight over Claude Mythos 5
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Anthropic faced a US export control directive regarding its latest AI models, Mythos 5 and Fable 5.
The directive required the company to suspend access to the new models.
The conflict highlights geopolitical and regulatory hurdles facing advanced AI deployment in the US.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
It is categorized under text-generation and utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.
The model is associated with the DeepSeek_v3 architecture, indicating relevance within the LLM space.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating its generative capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also delivers faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Apple devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing on-device, private AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Fox wants to take over your TV — and the tech inside it
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Fox is acquiring Roku, a major shift in the streaming technology landscape.
The deal is valued at $22 billion and targets Roku's position as a streaming portal.
This acquisition impacts over 100 million homes globally, affecting how consumers access streaming services.

stories.title: All the news about Anthropic’s new AI fight with the White House
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: Anthropic is facing a new government dispute involving the Pentagon.
The White House issued an order restricting foreign access to Anthropic's new AI models (Fable 5 and Mythos 5).
The models were recently launched, with Anthropic claiming Fable 5 has advanced capabilities.

stories.title: Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic temporarily shut down its newest, most powerful AI models following a request from the White House.
The US government reportedly demanded that Anthropic block access to its AI models for all foreign nationals, including its own staff.
The incident is framed by the article as evidence supporting the case for non-American AI development.

stories.title: UK to ban social media for kids under 16, may impose overnight curfews
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The UK is considering banning social media access for children under the age of 16.
The proposed legislation may also include the imposition of overnight curfews for minors.
Critics argue that such bans could drive children toward riskier online alternatives or circumvent restrictions using VPNs.

stories.title: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25B in first bond deal since 2021
stories.author: Michelle Chan and Tim Bradshaw, Financial Times
bullet_points: Nvidia is planning a significant debt issuance, aiming to raise over $25 billion.
The bond deal is being analyzed as a test of investor appetite for further AI sector exposure.
The move comes amid a current 'deluge of borrowing' within the technology industry.

stories.title: Fox’s $22B Roku acquisition aims to expand its reach into smart TVs, advertising
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Fox is planning a major acquisition of Roku valued at $22 billion.
The acquisition aims to significantly expand Fox's presence in the smart TV and streaming market.
Fox intends to gain control over Roku's streaming hardware, operating system, and FAST services.

stories.title: TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: TokenPilot addresses the high inference costs associated with context accumulation in long-horizon LLM agent sessions.
It tackles the problem where traditional context management (pruning/eviction) causes cache invalidation due to sequence mutations.
The framework introduces a dual-granularity approach to balance text sparsity with prompt cache continuity.

stories.title: ActiveSAM: Image-Conditional Class Pruning for Fast and Accurate Open-Vocabulary Segmentation
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: ActiveSAM addresses the inefficiency of applying large models like SAM 3 to Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation (OVSS).
It introduces a training-free, zero-shot inference framework to convert SAM 3 into an active-vocabulary segmenter.
The core innovation involves pruning the decoding process to only focus on the small, active subset of classes present in each image.

stories.title: Your Privacy My Cloak: Backdoor Attacks on Differentially Private Federated Learning
stories.author: Wenhai Sun
bullet_points: Challenges the assumption that Differential Privacy (DP) inherently protects Federated Learning (FL) from backdoor attacks.
Finds a fundamental tension where DP compliance masks the statistical signatures of malicious updates.
Suggests that while bypassing DP allows current defenses to work, DP itself can inadvertently hide threats.

stories.title: TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: TokenPilot addresses the rising inference costs in LLM agents due to context accumulation in long-horizon sessions.
It tackles the problem where traditional context management methods (pruning/eviction) cause cache invalidation and prefix mismatches.
The framework introduces a dual-granularity context management system to balance text sparsity with prompt cache continuity.

stories.title: The Value Axis: Language Models Encode Whether They're on the Right Track
stories.author: Jack Lindsey
bullet_points: Researchers investigated if Language Models (LMs) internally track the 'value' of their current operational trajectory.
A 'value' axis was constructed for Qwen3-8B using synthetic in-context reinforcement learning data.
Activations along this axis correlate with model performance, distinguishing between high/low confidence, correct/corrupted code, and backtracking behavior.

stories.title: Context-Aware RL for Agentic and Multimodal LLMs
stories.author: Xingyu Fu
bullet_points: Introduces ContextRL, a new context-aware Reinforcement Learning (RL) method for LLMs.
Addresses a key LLM weakness: failing to identify decisive evidence within long or complex contexts (e.g., tool traces, images).
Improves long-horizon reasoning and multimodal performance by using an indirect auxiliary objective instead of only supervising the final answer.

stories.title: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Meta-Analysis Articles from Nature Portfolio
stories.author: Qingyao Ai
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaSyn,' a new dataset of 442 expert-curated meta-analyses from Nature Portfolio journals.
The dataset is designed to benchmark Large Language Model (LLM) agents on complex, multi-stage scientific reasoning tasks.
It addresses a gap in current benchmarks by providing ground truth across the entire literature retrieval, screening, and synthesis pipeline.

stories.title: TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: Addresses the high inference costs associated with context accumulation in long-horizon LLM agent sessions.
Introduces TokenPilot, a dual-granularity context management framework to improve efficiency.
Solves a critical trade-off between achieving text sparsity and maintaining prompt cache continuity, which is disrupted by existing pruning methods.

stories.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
stories.author: lwhsiao
bullet_points: A security vulnerability (backdoor) was discovered within a LinkedIn job offer.
This represents a significant security risk targeting professional networking and recruitment platforms.
The incident highlights ongoing threats related to social engineering and platform vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for improved functionality on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local execution on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Facebook’s new AI Mode search gets its info from public posts
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Meta is rolling out a new 'AI Mode' search feature on Facebook.
This AI Mode utilizes data from users' public Facebook posts to generate search results.
The rollout is part of broader AI feature updates, including new photo presets.

stories.title: Xbox is closing down Hellblade creator Ninja Theory
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Xbox is reportedly closing down Ninja Theory, the developer of the Hellblade series.
Staff members were notified of the closure via a call on Monday.
The closure aligns with broader restructuring at Microsoft, affecting several Xbox studios.

stories.title: Google Chrome is closing the loopholes that let old ad blockers keep working
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google Chrome is planning to eliminate support for legacy ad blocker workarounds in versions 150 and 151.
This update targets the final remaining methods that allowed older ad blockers to function within Chrome.
The move follows the earlier phase-out of Manifest V2-based ad-blocking extensions in 2024.

stories.title: A Chinese rocket breaks apart dangerously close to the Starlink constellation
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: A Chinese rocket experienced a dangerous breakup near the Starlink constellation.
The incident is estimated to have created between 100 and 150 new pieces of space debris.
The event highlights ongoing risks associated with space debris and satellite constellations.

stories.title: Users cry foul after AMD stripped memory crypto from its consumer CPUs
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: AMD has removed TSME (Total Memory Encryption) from its consumer CPUs.
The removal is suggested by the article to be a deliberate and potentially covert corporate decision.
The change has drawn negative reactions and criticism from users.

stories.title: 20 years of Intel Macs: Why Apple switched, and why it switched again
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: The article reviews the entire lifecycle of Intel Macs over the past two decades.
It analyzes the strategic reasons behind Apple's decision to transition away from Intel processors.
The piece discusses the implications and history of Apple's subsequent hardware shifts.

stories.title: The Importance of Phase in Neural Representations: An Internal Oppenheim-Lim Test of Image Classifiers
stories.author: Alper Yıldırım
bullet_points: The research investigates whether modern neural networks (PRISM2D, GFNet, ViT-B/16) exhibit the phase-sensitivity asymmetry observed in natural images.
It tests the hypothesis, based on Oppenheim-Lim (1981), that image identity is more encoded in the phase of its Fourier representation than its magnitude.
The study uses a causal intervention method, transplanting phases between images, to determine which input the classifier's prediction follows.

stories.title: FusionRS: A Large-Scale RGB-Infrared Remote Sensing Dataset for Dual-Modal Vision-Language Foundation Models
stories.author: Jiujiang Guo
bullet_points: Introduces FusionRS, a new large-scale dataset combining RGB and Infrared remote sensing imagery with text.
Addresses a gap in current remote sensing AI by integrating infrared data, which offers thermal and illumination-invariant features.
Aims to advance dual-modal vision-language foundation models for improved Earth observation understanding.

stories.title: TuneJury: An Open Metric for Improving Music Generation Preference Alignment
stories.author: Chris Donahue
bullet_points: Introduces TuneJury, an open, instance-level pairwise reward model for text-to-music generation.
The model predicts a music preference score based on a text prompt and an audio clip.
It is trained on diverse public human preference labels, including A vs. B votes and expert ratings.

stories.title: Bayesian Inference and Decision Audits for Public Archives of Frontier AI Evaluations
stories.author: Yanan Long
bullet_points: The paper critiques the common practice of viewing public AI evaluations as simple, terminal leaderboards.
It frames the collection of various AI benchmarks (LiveBench, Open LLM Leaderboard v2, etc.) as a complex Bayesian inference problem.
The research suggests that evaluation results are heavily influenced by reporting rules, benchmark revisions, and data missingness, necessitating deeper longitudinal analysis.

stories.title: When in Doubt, Plan It Out: Committed Small Language Model Deliberation for Reactive Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Odinaldo Rodrigues
bullet_points: Introduces PACT, a hybrid architecture designed to improve Reinforcement Learning (RL) robustness in unfamiliar environments.
Combines a fast, reactive RL policy with a slow, deliberative Small Language Model (SLM) planner for explicit planning.
The SLM generates and validates action plans via simulation before they are executed, bypassing the standard RL policy.

stories.title: Geometric Action Model for Robot Policy Learning
stories.author: Sunghwan Hong
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in current Vision-Language-Action (VLA) and World-Action Models (WAMs) by focusing on 3D geometry.
Proposes a 'Geometric Action Model' to enable generalist robot policies to reason about 3D physical interactions.
Crucial for advancing contact-rich manipulation tasks where 2D representations are insufficient.

stories.title: Hierarchical Advantage Weighting for Online RL Fine-Tuning of VLAs from Sparse Episode Outcomes
stories.author: Hongsheng Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse binary outcomes (success/failure) in online Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of Vision-Language Agents (VLAs).
Critiques existing methods that reduce sparse outcomes to a single scalar reward, which conflates necessary transition-level feedback.
Proposes a new 'Hierarchical Advantage Weighting' mechanism to provide richer, more granular guidance during the fine-tuning process.

stories.title: The Importance of Phase in Neural Representations: An Internal Oppenheim-Lim Test of Image Classifiers
stories.author: Alper Yıldırım
bullet_points: The research investigates whether modern neural network classifiers (PRISM2D, GFNet, ViT-B/16) exhibit the phase-sensitivity asymmetry observed in natural images.
It tests the hypothesis, based on Oppenheim-Lim (1981), that image identity is encoded more in the phase of its Fourier representation than its magnitude.
The study uses a causal intervention technique—transplanting phase between images—to determine which input the classifier's prediction follows.

stories.title: KVEraser: Learning to Steer KV Cache for Efficient Localized Context Erasing
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: Addresses the difficulty of post-hoc context erasing in LLMs due to the global propagation of cached states.
Proposes 'KVEraser' to efficiently steer the KV cache for localized context removal.
Relevant for long-context LLM applications where stale facts or harmful injections require mid-stream correction.

stories.title: ExpRL: Exploratory RL for LLM Mid-Training
stories.author: Aviral Kumar
bullet_points: Introduces ExpRL, a new approach using Exploratory Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM improvement during mid-training.
Addresses a limitation of current RL fine-tuning, which relies on manually specifying primitive reasoning skills (e.g., decomposition, self-correction).
Aims to improve LLM reasoning by exploring capabilities rather than relying solely on curated, manually defined training traces.

stories.title: KVEraser: Learning to Steer KV Cache for Efficient Localized Context Erasing
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of post-hoc context erasing in LLMs due to the global propagation of cached states.
Proposes 'KVEraser' to efficiently steer the KV cache for localized context removal.
Relevant for long-context LLM applications where stale or harmful information needs to be corrected after processing.

stories.title: DEEPRUBRIC: Evidence-Tree Rubric Supervision for Efficient Reinforcement Learning of Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Jiyan He
bullet_points: Introduces DEEPRUBRIC, a method using Evidence-Tree Rubric Supervision to improve Deep Research Agents.
The research addresses the efficiency challenge in RL-based research agents by using rubric-based rewards for quality assessment.
It tackles the limitation of current methods where LLMs generate rubrics that may fail to capture the necessary underlying information.

stories.title: IMPACTeen: Intentions, Manipulation, Persuasion, Annotations, and Consequences in Teen Communication Dataset
stories.author: Przemysław Kazienko
bullet_points: Introduces IMPACTeen, a novel dataset focusing on social influence in adolescent communication.
The dataset is rich, containing 1,021 texts and 5,100 annotations, labeled from five distinct expert perspectives.
It addresses a niche but critical area of AI/NLP: modeling intentions, manipulation, and persuasion in youth digital interactions.

stories.title: LESS Is More: Mutual-Stability Sampling for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Michalis Vazirgiannis
bullet_points: Introduces LESS, a training-free sampling method designed to improve the efficiency of Diffusion Language Models (dLLMs).
dLLMs are presented as a promising alternative to traditional autoregressive decoding, allowing for parallel and bidirectional token updates.
The core problem addressed is the inefficiency in current dLLM sampling, where computation is wasted on already-stable or prematurely committed tokens.

stories.title: BrowserAct vs Playwright: Where Test Automation Hits Real-World Anti-Bot Friction (Hands-On Comparison)
stories.author: hadil
bullet_points: Compares two tools, BrowserAct and Playwright, in the context of real-world anti-bot challenges.
Addresses the common gap between local testing success and CI/production environment failures due to bot detection.
Provides a hands-on comparison relevant to QA engineers and automation practitioners.
